payload:
# PROMPT.md — Model Switching

## Vision

Users can swap the active AI model mid-session without restarting `ap`. A `/model <name>` slash command switches the model for all subsequent turns. The current model is shown in the TUI status bar and in `--prompt` mode output. Config supports a default model via `[provider] model = "..."` in `~/.ap/config.toml`.

## Requirements

### 1. Config: default model
- Add `model` field to `[provider]` section in `Config` struct (`src/config.rs`)
- Default: `... [truncated, 1999 chars total]

task_id: task-1774277106-9f74, task_key: pdd:model-switching:requirements — Q1: Should the model be passed as a per-call parameter to stream_completion() (TuiApp.model_name as single source of truth, threaded down each turn), or should BedrockProvider own the model with interior mutability (Mutex<String> + set_model method) to allow switching via Arc<dyn Provider>?

Q1 answered: use per-call model parameter on stream_completion() — Conversation.model is the natural carrier, threads through turn() → stream_completion(model, ...), no interior mutability needed. task_id=task-1774277106-9f74 task_key=pdd:model-switching:requirements

model-switching requirements complete: per-call model param on stream_completion(), Conversation.model as carrier, /model slash command, --model CLI flag, config default us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6-v1:0. task_id=task-1774277106-9f74 task_key=pdd:model-switching:requirements

model-switching design complete: per-call model param on stream_completion(), Conversation.model as carrier, /model slash command in TUI, --model CLI flag, config default updated to us.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-6-v1:0. task_id=task-1774277481-e61d task_key=pdd:model-switching:design-draft

model-switching design rejected: ChatEntry::System variant referenced in sections 4.4 and 7.1 does not exist in the actual enum (variants are User, AssistantStreaming, AssistantDone, ToolCall). Design must specify either (a) add new ChatEntry::System(String) variant, or (b) use existing AssistantDone variant for slash command feedback. Fix this gap then resubmit. task_id=task-1774277608-9b63 task_key=pdd:model-switching:design-review

Q2: For the /model confirmation message, should we add a new ChatEntry::System(String) variant (new enum variant + rendering branch, visually distinct from assistant) OR reuse ChatEntry::AssistantDone (no enum change, but looks identical to AI output)? task_id=task-1774277608-9b63 task_key=pdd:model-switching:design-review

Q2 answered: Add ChatEntry::System(String) variant with theme.muted rendering (not AssistantDone reuse). Design updated: sections 4.4-4.5 rewritten, data model diagram updated, impl sequence expanded to 9 steps. task_id=task-1774277608-9b63 task_key=pdd:model-switching:design-review

Both blocking Q&A resolved: (1) per-call model param on stream_completion(), (2) new ChatEntry::System(String) variant with theme.muted rendering. Design.md fully updated with 9-step impl sequence. task_id=task-1774277608-9b63 task_key=pdd:model-switching:design-review

Model switching design complete: 9-step impl sequence, per-call model param on stream_completion(), new ChatEntry::System(String) variant with theme.muted rendering, --model CLI flag. task_id=task-1774277608-9b63 task_key=pdd:model-switching:design-review

Model-switching design approved. Per-call model param on stream_completion(), Conversation.model carrier, new ChatEntry::System(String) variant with theme.muted rendering, --model CLI flag. 9-step impl sequence verified against codebase. task_id=task-1774277608-9b63 task_key=pdd:model-switching:design-review

Explorer complete. 8 stream_completion impls found. Critical gap: context.rs/summarise_messages also calls stream_completion — needs model param + 2 test providers updated. /model intercept must come before User push in handle_submit. 226 tests passing. task_id=task-1774278032-57cd task_key=pdd:model-switching:research
Session Management UX fully implemented: auto-named sessions, --prompt saves to ~/.ap/sessions/<name>/, -s resumes, --list-sessions lists. 226 tests pass, cargo build clean.
